interventions.intervention_text:
[Investigation Advisor — v4] phase=mid primary=M6 secondary=M5
You've done a lot of work tracing errors through several services and have arrived at a candidate explanation. Before committing, pause on two things.

First, you've queried exclusively within the incident window — you haven't compared any of this data against a normal period. Some of what you're seeing (certain error patterns, connection warnings, queue-related messages) might be chronic background noise rather than the trigger for this specific incident. Have you confirmed that the patterns you're treating as causal are actually elevated compared to baseline?

Second, one of your candidate explanations involves a component that doesn't appear in your trace data at all — it only shows up in log messages from other services. Absence from traces isn't the same as absence from the system; it could also mean that component stopped emitting telemetry entirely, or was never instrumented. Is the silence itself a signal worth examining more carefully?
